problem_statement: Nested Mind exposes a hash-bound GitHub check-run writer pattern, while Mullu Control Plane has no local receipt boundary that can plan or bind readiness evidence to GitHub Checks without granting direct GitHub write authority. This creates a gap between local SDLC proof receipts and future external check-run publication.
success_criteria: GitHub check-run write plans build deterministic REST endpoint and request payload evidence for repository, head SHA, check name, status, conclusion, and output fields
Plan-only and dry-run modes perform no GitHub network call, authenticate no request, and cannot claim response check-run evidence
Write-approved mode requires operator approval, GitHub App installation id, external execution receipt ref, response check-run id, and response payload hash before an external write is admitted
Secret-shaped material in metadata or payload fields blocks receipt admission with explicit causal reasons
The check-run write receipt is schema-backed, indexed in the governance protocol manifest, and included in proof coverage matrix witnesses
No GitHub App token minting, GitHub API mutation, branch protection mutation, deployment, or production readiness claim is introduced
constraints: all write-approved claims must bind external execution evidence instead of performing the network call in the planner
plan-only and dry-run modes must remain free of external effects
response payload hashes must be sha256-prefixed hex digests
receipt generation must be deterministic and schema-valid
evidence_required: standalone SDLC requirement and design validation receipt
workspace governance preflight receipt before closure claim
